rulings.Case Title: YINGQIAN WANG VS. IAN PHELPS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, line 7, DEFENDANT IAN PHELPS MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIORE CATTARUZZA VS. ACRO-SPORT GYMNASTICS & SPORTS ACROBATICS CENTER, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues As To Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, line 6, DEFENDANTS ACRO-SPORT GYMNASTICS & SPORTS ACROBATICS CENTER, , DBA ACROSPORTS, ALICIA HOFFMAN, DORRIE HUNTINGTON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Issues As To Plaintiffs Complaint *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Defendants' motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternate motion is denied as the first cause of action for negligence/gross negligence and granted as to both defendants Acro-Sports and Huntington as to the second cause of action for negligent hiring, supervision, and retention. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BIKOFF VS. LIONTREE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, line 1, DEFENDANTS NICHOLAS TUOSTO, BEN BRAUN, LIONTREE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions Defendant's motion to compel further discovery responses is denied. The parties agree that the motion to compel deadline (as extended) was on October 17, 2024. (Plaintiff's Opposing P&A, 5:1-3; Defendant's Reply P&A, 3:5-6.) The record shows that defendant filed this motion on October 21, 2024. Since defendant missed the 45-day period in which to file the motion, it is denied. (Sexton v. Superior Court (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 1403, 1410 [court lacks authority to rule on an untimely motion]; Vidal Sassoon, Inc. v. Superior Court (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 681, 685 [time limitations to bring a motion to compel are mandatory].) For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENDY TELLO ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, line 2, 2 - PLAINTIFFS KENDY TELLO, ARIEL TELLO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to their set one requests for production of documents is granted. No later than February 14, 2025 on a mutually agreeable date or dates American Honda must produce all non-privileged responsive documents. American Honda must identify any documents withheld on the grounds of privilege in a privilege log. The document requests meet the standard for discovery relevance and American Honda has not shown that compliance with the requests is unduly burdensome. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENDY TELLO ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, January 14, 2025, line 2, 1 - PLAINTIFFS KENDY TELLO, ARIEL TELLO'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents Plaintiffs' motion to compel PMK deposition and production of documents is granted. No later than February 14, 2025 on a mutually agreeable date or dates American Honda must make available for deposition one or more PMKs and produce all non-privileged responsive documents. American Honda must identify any documents withheld on the grounds of privilege in a privilege log. Plaintiffs are entitled to a PMK deposition, the matters for examination and document requests meet the standard for discovery relevance, and American Honda has not shown that compliance with the notice of deposition is unduly burdensome. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)
